reviews.review_text:
Outer Wilds — One of the Best Exploration Games I’ve Played

What I loved most about Outer Wilds was the freedom it gives you. You start with nothing but your spaceship and are free to explore however you want — no objectives, no map markers, just pure curiosity driving the adventure. Every planet feels unique and full of mystery, and discovering their secrets was incredibly rewarding.

This is one of the few sci-fi games that genuinely made me feel like I was in space. The atmosphere and sense of isolation are amazing, and I had countless jaw-dropping moments while exploring.

I also really liked how progression is entirely based on knowledge instead of upgrades or skill trees. The more you learn, the more the world starts to make sense, which made every discovery feel meaningful.

Simply put, Outer Wilds is a once-in-a-generation game which should be experienced by everyone.

I want to start this review off by admitting I did not like Outer Wilds. I normally love things in this field, but for some reason it just wasn't my bag.  

Spoiler free- That being said, I want to give it a positive review because its an incredibly niche and very well built game. All of the things I didn't like are probably pros to others, and I can't deny that it's good and achieves what it sets out to. If space exploration and really working to uncover a story bit by bit, along side with some fairly challenging puzzles (by that I mean really paying attention and finding clues that may or may not tie into something you're supposed to piece together) then this will probably be a wonderful adventure for you. 

If you're one of the rare people who, like me, find yourself not enjoying it after several hours then it probably wont improve with more time invested. I only say that because I kept digging through it trying to figure out how and why this game was so overwhelmingly spoken of as a masterpiece, and I never got there. I felt let down. But I really think that's just because Its about personal taste, not because Outer Wilds was lacking. Where some games are just bad and I am completely willing to trash on them, I have a soft spot for games with real vision and a truly curated experience. Outer Wilds is good, and if it sounds like your thing, you'll probably love it.
Just trust me and buy it, it's worth your money. The less you know about the game going into it, the more fun you'll have

One of the best games I've ever played. My favorite part was when [spoiler] BUY IT NOW [/spoiler] and then the [spoiler] I CANT STRESS THIS ENOUGH BUY IT NOW [/spoiler]. Overall It's a really great game

Outer wilds just captures your attention from the start till the credits roll, and there is no dull moment. It is kind of game that when credits roll, you're just stunned that what you just played and you feel hollow that it has ended. It is like a journey which you never want to end.  One of the rare occasions where mystery element is done right in games. This game gives you freedom in every way, exploration is open ended and never gatekeeps you from getting creative with it, only limitation is your knowledge. First blind playthrough of this game is something special, so don't spoil it by looking up stuff about this game, even the minor details can fade the fun experience. It rewards curiosity, exploration and how you use your tools. Really great game and I wholeheartedly recommend it.
Loved it. Great music. Interesting approach to storytelling. It's an art piece. Not many games like that exist.

Don't trust your friends that keep recommending this game to you, they're actually doing it for an incredibly sinister and selfish reason - to relive the magical feeling it gives you the first time you make your way through it, using you as the vessel for that
One hell of a journey. It can be jarring at first as it's very open ended and non linear but anyone into space stuff should look into giving it a shot. Chefs kiss on the story and world design.

[h1]I Tried[/h1]
I've tried to get into this game about 3 times now and it just feels like one of those old moon logic adventure games at times, and not in the good way. The moment a few hours in that I realized [spoiler]Planets change as the loop progresses[/spoiler] I was hype at the amazing idea and the possibities for exploration! It was not long before my heart sank as I realized how tedious the exploration to find out anything at all actually was going to be.

You have to work hard to get scraps of info and after 15 hours of this I have a few threads left to follow but big picture I have no idea what is going on with the story. I have bits and peices sure but on their own they are just puzzle peices that mean nothing without the rest of the puzzle. My main issue with actually playing is my efforts to engage with the universe are met with arduously repeating the same steps over and over for hours literally on a loop looking for bits of text to scan. It's like looking for audio logs in other games but there is no audio and the logs are 80% of the story and the story is not compelling and the writers of the logs are not memorable or interesting in any way.

The controls are a bit janky all around as well, since the game is about wandering around in your ship or with your jetpack, the fact the physics feel so bouncy and controls so clunky is a rather large issue. On controller or mouse/kb neither feels good I can't put my finger on it but the movement both in ship and on foot feels like you are piloting a glass beachball with thrusters.

I think the idea of the game is amazing, but at a certain point I just don't care why anything is happening because every attempt to find out is met with another tedious puzzle to find out info about unlikable dead aliens and a thread to follow for yet another tedious puzzle. It feels very nihilistic.

It's really a puzzle/adventure game with all the baggage that entails. If you want to engage with it on it's terms, then I'm sure it feels great, but I found it so boring and tedious I didn't even find out the whys of the story and I'm ok with that cuz I bet everyone dies anyway.

This isn't a bad game by any means, but it's just not for me. Outer Wilds is basically Myst in space, which in theory sounds cool, and there are some interesting things about the game, but I really didn't think I was going to despise the time loop as much as I did.

This is a puzzle game that encourages exploration and tinkering, to reveal not only the story but the world that was built. In Myst, you'd take that at your leisure. In this game, you spend a lot of time preparing, reviewing notes, flying around, and very little actually progressing the game before the time loop gets you. This just caused me to rush everything I did, speed through dialogue, and just generally stress out. I feel like I should have had the time to admire and learn. It broke my immersion.

While the story was interesting and the game is beautiful, you kind of learn the gist of what's happening after a couple loops, and after that it just became tedious doing similar things over and over to reveal smaller details, wasting time getting from destination to destination, racing to meet specifically timed objectives, and getting past a couple obtuse puzzles that frankly would have benefited the player having more time to simmer over. I got bored.

Wound up looking up what was needed to do the ending since I was pretty close, saw the frankly ridiculous series of events, said no thank you, and uninstalled. Probably got through about 80% of it. Yeah, you get your journal notes, and they're pretty good, but I don't need the stress of trying to cram all that into a single loop.

DO IT!! PLAY IT!! This is a game you want to go into as blind as possible as in a game like this the fun of it is exploring and putting the answers to the puzzles and figuring out what is happening is the fun of it. From dialogue with characters that changes over time to finding ruins and text and how they all correlate to help you with figuring out one of your goals. 

If you are like me and love story and having to think on what you're learning and how it all works then this is for you. Just keep in mind that any spoilers you gain may change you're experience and that everyone plays in different ways but no matter what, this is a game that the main bulk of the world can only be experienced once. 

I currently have a low playtime as I had a larger save on ps4 and wanted to experience the game again and support the devs.
Fantastic game, best to go in with no prior knowledge if possible. Captures a sense of wonder and adventure that words are incapable of describing. It's an experience that everyone should have.

I can't write a review for this game that would actually detail its excellence without spoiling the going-in-blind experience, so I'll just describe this game in a few sentences.

Outer Wilds is [i]one of the best video games ever made[/i] and it will change the way you think about video games forever. In an age where so many games have devolved into having an obsession with holding the player's hand because of an intense fear of them not enjoying the game correctly, whether due to fear of backlash from a vocal minority that nobody's actually listening to, or simply from trying to mask lazy game design, Outer Wilds is a wonderful breath of fresh air. Go ahead, get lost and confused in this world and do as you please, because no matter what happens, this game will always trust you and never insult your intelligence.
It's a kind of game you want to forget, just so that you can play it again with the same level of excitement. What an amazing story! And the physics of the game are a class apart. 10/10 !

I've never played a game that felt like it was made 100% for people like me. If you are a curious person, if you like exploring, or if you like figuring stuff out on your own, then I am sure that you will feel the same way.

I played this game 5 years ago, I was 15, right after moving from Monterrey, Mexico, to Arizona, United States, I found this game because of youtube, I saw a video of almost 2 hours by Joseju which caught my attention, at the beginning of the video he recommended everyone to play the game before watching the video, not as a traditional spoiler alert, it seemed as something more important than that, my intuition told me to go and play the game, so I asked my mother for 10 dollars to buy it, I started it that same night, I have always been an enthusiast of astronomy and physics, so when I discovered I could fly a spaceship with realistic physics, planet gravitational pulls, orbits and gravities I was suprised by the technical details put in this game, with the more I played it I discovered that was the least impressive thing of this experience. I fell in love with the exploration, learning about the Nomai's, learning about what had happened to the other explorers, learning how the galaxy we get to explore works, but most importantly, learn that things do not happen because of me, but because they are the way the are. 

I discovered this game a moment of my life where I was lost, physically in a new place, socially in a new place, I did not even know how to speak the language and I still kind of do not know how to do so, but when I finished this game, I cried, so much, the realization that we are really just a small thing in the big scheme of things really put me into perspective, I always have known this, but is not something you can wrap your mind around really, at least I was never able to feel that way until I finished this game and had that experience from it. Since that moment I started to realize how a lot of my worries and stressors were really not that big of a deal, which might sound bad and sad, but in reality it put my mind and energy into another perspective.

Thanks to Outer Wilds I learned that no matter where I am, with who, when, no matter the situation I have to go through, the best thing I can do is slow down and enjoy it,  appreciate the good, the bad, the worst and the best because one day, today will be the past and the future will be built over that past that today is today,  until the day there is no tomorrow, because I know that I do not want to look and wish I enjoyed life more, I want to look back and be happy that I stopped to smell the trees along the way. 

I found this game when I was lost in live to show me how to find my way back to myself. 
This game found me again 5 years later, when I need a reminder that everything will be fine.

Outer Wilds is a game about the wonders of space exploration and uncovering the secrets of an ancient, extinct race called the Nomai. You are an astronaut for Outer Wilds Ventures, and your goal is to understand the secrets of your solar system by translating text, uncovering patterns, and learning fundamental rules of existence.

This game challenges you to use your brain by encouraging you to be interested in discovery and learning pieces of information at a time. You create a mental model of how all these strange machines work, and how everything-- and I mean EVERYTHING-- in the solar system is connected.

This game was filled with moments that made me feel like a child filled with utter fascination. It all makes you question how things came to be how they are now. That sense of wonder is what is so compelling about it.

I cannot speak directly about the game's contents. Doing so would be a disservice to anybody looking to play through it blind. All I can say is that everything is connected and every puzzle has a solution that you can reason through. The game's ending, in particular, shocked me to my core, and I hold Outer Wilds as one of the best experiences of my life.

If you enjoy video games, or narrative games, or games with an interesting story, then play Outer Wilds. It isn't too long of an experience, and you may find that it will change your life the way it has changed mine.

Grate game, but challenging
requires to use your brain a lot
Perhaps the most impressive achievement of this game is that it runs so well on my poor hardware! A real masterpiece.

This Game is a straight 10 out of 10 if you like Exploration games.
One of those games where you give other Indie games a try aswell just because this one showed you that a great Game can come from anywhere.
The Soundtrack brings tears to you eyes afterwards because it reminds you of the story and the journey you had through the game.
The DLC adds to the whole game aswell.

Just a pure 10/10 for those who can enjoy exploration/story rich games.

I picked up this game a few years ago and dropped it pretty quickly after finding it kind of boring. I'm quite thankful that I didn't play the game then, as I definitely wasn't ready for the message it was going to give me. It's a beautiful game about true exploration. No quests, no maps, no guides, just simple curiosity and intuition. I don't think it's being dramatic to say that this game changed how I view life. You'll definitely run into some frustrating puzzles, but just remember to stop and smell the pine trees along the way :)
Outer Wilds is the kind of game you hope to live long enough to forget enough about to play it fresh a second time.

Outer Wilds is one of the first games in a while that I've read so much good about that I only waited for a –40 % sale before buying it (and then it went for –50 % just before I actually played it – curses!). And indeed, what could be more intriguing than Groundhog Day in SPACE?! That's all I knew before diving in.

Besides the soothing theme song, the first thing I noticed upon starting was the lack of voice acting, which came as a bit of a surprise. Though I immediately recalled my playthrough of Stray last year, so I guess voicelessness seems to be a thing to expect in Annapurna's titles. Seeing as there's only a handful of NPCs that you spend a fraction of your time interacting with, it really isn't a thing that anyone should get hung up on. Despite the few interactions, the NPCs where quite a nice cast with memorable, distinct personalities.

Outer Wilds is a fresh take on the exploration genre where you start out with all the tools you'll need, but without all the information. Instead of waiting until late-game to upgrade your pickaxe to unlock a new shortcut, the shortcut is already there – you just most likely won't see it hiding in plain sight! The only downside to this approach is that like an escape room, you can only experience Outer Wilds once. That's why I highly encourage new players to take their time and figure everything out on their own!

The world-building in Outer Wilds is incredibly well done, and exploring the solar system is thus extremely rewarding. Though there is an amount of suspension of disbelief required with some aspects – as you'll most likely see – even though the planets are compellingly designed and conceptually cool. Exploring the world is in and of itself a big puzzle with multiple moving parts. Freely roaming in a big sandbox allows you to choose what parts to focus on when connecting the dots and to organically change what planet you're focusing on every twenty minutes for example, which keeps things fresh and lessens the odds of getting stuck. I also really appreciated that you can see hints for places that have more to explore and an overview for how things are connected. Personally, I was merely whelmed by the ending, which slightly redacted points from the whole, even though the process of discovery might be flawless. There's an optional goal and a DLC that have an extremely minor effect on the ending as well.

I had a tough time deciding on whether to use controller or mouse & keyboard when starting, but decided to go with controller, since that's what the developers have intended – and I usually aim to see their vision to fruition. In the end I think both have their merits: steering your spaceship might be a tad more fun with twin-sticks and triggers, but navigating the world in first-person will probably always be smoother with a mouse. Both should work perfectly well, nonetheless. The controls were quite intuitive, and you can even switch what input device you're using and have the on-screen prompts instantly change to match. There were also some controls like rolling with your ship and ejecting out of the cockpit that I somehow managed to disregard during my whole playthrough, which made life a tad harder – don't be like me!

At first I wasn't even sure what part of the game the DLC was supposed to be, though I had a(n accurate) suspicion. The DLC differs slightly from the main game in that it essentially takes place in one location, and is seen as having more of a horror spin. Although personally, I found the main game to be much more terrifying, since nothing beats the horror that is SPACE (except maybe the depths of the ocean – time to play Subnautica soon...). I still get a feeling of dread and stop breathing every time I notice I'm unexpectedly drifting too fast towards the sun.

At first the DLC felt huge with explorable branches here and there, but after some red herrings it suddenly came to an end sooner than I expected. Being forced into one location made the experience a bit more repetitive with having to fly to the same place over and over, but since Echoes of the Eye isn't ultimately that long, it's manageable. For me the DLC had many more frustrating moments than the base game, which ultimately led me to look up part of a solution for one puzzle (the only time in the whole game) after hitting my head against a wall for a sufficient amount of time. In the end it was a pretty good DLC that some people are bound to like more than others.

Outer Wilds is definitively an outlier in the achievements department: after my 50-hour playthrough of the base game and the DLC I was still sitting at a miniscule 3/31 total achievements! After beating the game I pulled up the list and noticed that none of them were exactly tied to organic gameplay or advancing the story. Most of them were related to dying in some novel way or doing something whacky you already know isn't going to work. Except for a couple of annoying ones the achievements in this one were pretty fun to do after finishing the game, and provided 9 hours of additional gameplay.

All in all, Outer Wilds is a unique explorational sandbox puzzle game with some horror elements and a whole lot of space. I can strongly recommend it to every explorer out there who likes to stop and smell the flowers along the way, and who aren't completely averse to puzzles and a bit of time pressure. 

[h1]10- / 10[/h1]
This is the best game ever. Don't stop yourself from playing it because "it's not replayable" or whatever, just play the game. DO IT

What can I say that hasn't been. This game made me feel every emotion I thought I had and then some. One of the first videogames I've played that I truly felt like I was completely engulfed in the world, vibes, and characters.

Every decision they made seemed intentional and when it's all laid out in front of you, you feel it all. When it finally clicked and I sat through the closing of the game I didn't even know what to think. One of those that you stare at the credits as they roll.

You wake up and you know absolutely nothing in a universe that basically tells you "go find out what you need to find out." If that sounds like your kind of game then stop reading and buy it. Most rewarding game I've ever played. 


The less you know from the start, the better. Buy this game

Wow, just wow. This game is one of a kind and plays with your mind so well. Its Puzzle atmosphere and scenes are amazing. I would highly recommend this game if your looking for an adventure with some surprises! really just do it!
Cool exploration game and beautiful art style, but that's about where the positives end. I found it very hard to get into and not a clue what i'm supposed to be doing.

This is the first time I'm leaving a public comment on a game, and it is probably the only time I will. This type of game is far removed from what I usually play, and yet it must be said : it's the best game I've ever played. I'm honored to have played it, proud to have solved it, and sad to leave it behind. Thank you for this.

"As a child, I considered such unknowns sinister. Now, though, I understand they bear no ill will. The universe is, and we are. I am ready."

what the ♥♥♥♥ peak best game of the loop

now I understand why this community can't speak about the this game!
highly recommend. 
if you want the ultimate experience, there's a voice mod that adds voices for the NPCs.
you will thank me!
If you have the patience and curiosity, and the balls to deals with the scares, it is a rewarding experience like no other.
Probably one of the best single player games anyone can play. I recommend getting dementia and writing down that you need to play this game on a sheet of paper somewhere so you can play it again.
One of my favorite gaming experiences ever. The things I'd do to experience this for the first time again... Please play this game, and don't look up a thing before you do. 100% recommend <3

I am among the many who revere this outstanding game as one of the best they have ever played. I can only hope that all games were crafted with such love and care... 

In the off chance that you, reader, are carelessly started reading reviews in order to decide whether or not you should play this game (even though you have already been adverted that you absolutely should go into the game blind), let me summarize in a spoiler-free manner a few of the aspects that you might find unfun, and give you some advice for free:

1. The base game might induce cosmic dread, and the DLC (Echoes of the Eye) might be frightening. The game is definitely not trying to get you to your nerves, and it is by no means a horror game, but it might demand you to have the guts to procede (for the lack of a better word). Go on exploring the solar system is definitvely an adventurous endeavor, but I definitely felt uneasiness when comtemplating the unknown and the unavoidable, the vast and the huge, and unknown and the unforgiving... But to me this is part of the core experience of the game, and it wouldn't be awesome without it. Overcome your fears! 
2. It can be a little difficult to get curious at first. The very first time you are playing this game, you should be aiming for a 45-minute session (or more), striving for not killing off your character with weird antics for at least that time, and using and trying all of the gadgets you will be given. 
3. There are a couple of big secrets awaiting for you, and you will be on your own to discover them. You might be among the unfortunate who do not get any big revelations for a *very* long gameplay time. And I am not kidding when I say "big revelations": the moment you get the first of those, you will be hooked. In the early game, you will get a lot of ideas as to what mysteries you can explore first, and you should pay attention to that. If you don't have better ideas, it can never be too bad to get to know the starting planet (Timber Hearth) and its natural satellite (the Attlerock) as the back of your hand. 
4. Controls, specially those concerning your ship's movements, are not easy to handle. I do not have any specific advice for you: just get good. I am not the most dexterous player myself, yet my skill was enough for everything I wanted to do. Please note that I do NOT mean that you have to "grind" or "level up" your character: this does not exist in the game. 
5.  Several important mechanics in the game are time-based and repetition-based. Some of these can (and will) become tedious as you progress through it. Please, preservere. Skill, smarts, and luck will be on your side eventually. 
6. You will often feel that the game is letting you know that you do not have whatever is required to progress through some section, to which I respond: the game is not telling you that. Sometimes you will feel you are not ready yet to do this or that, to which I say: you are indeed ready. Part of the appeal of the game is to try unorthodox ways to accomplish objectives!  

Be sure to enjoy every bit of it because it is a one-time experience.

Very unique game. I'm hooked on it. 

Its like if the boy scouts had a space program... And the solar system was only 50 miles wide. Curiosity fueled exploration.

It's also great to watch someone else play.

Very easy recommend. It's a special game. Regrettably I used a guide for one of the final lynchpin puzzles because I did the right thing, but not exactly right. It soured the experience a little, but not at all in a way that made me regret playing. It's come a long way from the little in-browser concept game I played 25 years ago

honestly the story did not hook me literally at all and i dont care for the puzzles, idk they just are not that interesting. Or maybe I didnt find the interesting ones. The reason my review is positive is that the planets themselves are just so unique and awesome just to maneuver on. There's not a lot of content here, but it's a truly unique world with some great physics going on.

an incredibly compelling story that explores such ambitious and interesting concepts - rather than being solely linear, the game is curiosity-driven as you, the player, drive exploration and discovery, leading you to find clues to the story of this ancient civilization. it's set in a very beautiful, rich, and vast world - also really love the soundtrack . 10/10 ending

Amazing game. I'll admit it took me a bit to get into it. But once I got going and finished the game, it was an immediate favorite. Like everyone says, the entire game is a spoiler. But you should play it, figure out why its such a secret. ;;)

Only 33% of players have the basic achievments that says a lot by itself. 

The game has clunky conrtols and require precision platforming, the physics are infuriating. I understand that it's a space game but in Prey, for example, the non-gravity sections were pretty fun. Here (especially on the smaller planets) you can kill yourself when the game switches from space to planet modes and calculate your velocity in a pretty obscure way.

The visual sequence after dying is very very long. One of the starting planets has a black hole that punishes any missed jump by making you repeat a teleporting sequence and getting back to the starting point. Also the game has a hidden timer so you can miss something on one of the planets without knowing it. So you gotta just go through the same empty location in different time sectors to hopefully find something.

Overall, it's a mess, feels more like a walking simulator/rage game hybrid. I don't really uderstand the hype, maybe it's one of them streamer games that people just love because they liked the content associated with it.

Also a billion notes does not count for world-building. All you do is reading notes and falling into the death traps.

Oh boy, it's time for another one of my reviews where I complain about a game everyone glazes and then proceed to still give it a 9. Don't blame me - this is how I can add value, when the merits of something have already been exposited on so thoroughly. This is reflective, so there won't be specific spoilers, but I'll talk about my experience and that might color yours. Probably don't read this if you're planning to play it.

Outer Wilds' gameplay loop is built around "set pieces" to reach reward areas. The set pieces are platforming/piloting challenges, simple riddles, traversal challenges requiring mastery of an area's gimmick, and a few stealth sections. Usually, several of those ingredients combine. I have two main gripes: the stealth sections are annoying to me and I hate them (this doesn't mean they are bad, just not to my taste) and the other sections are so transparently Video Game Levels with Game Design done on them that it hurts immersion. 

The reward areas mostly consist of reading, which is very condensed and similarly videogamey. It's hard to treat the ruins as a simulated relic and not a video game level when there's so much video game happening around you at all times. What I mean by that is that everything is placed conveniently where a player would want it and information is doled out to you in easily digestible tidbits that can fit into a one-line log entry. Any complexities of the alien technology are swept under the rug and add to the game's cartoony feel, which can be charming. This instance of immersion-breaking is more forgivable as it's a compromise they HAD to make for the game's core premise to function. If the investigation component was not so streamlined, people would get stuck everywhere and you'd need a far more overbearing support system to stop people from quitting.

The story is awesome and artistic on a theoretical level, but all the aforementioned immersion breaking meant it never struck me on an emotional level. So, while I followed along closely enough to see what the ending was going for, it fizzled anyway. I'm still left with an appreciation of the unique controls and map design that make this game what it is, and all the funny chaos it can cause if the player doesn't know exactly what can happen.

9/10

I tried liking it, I really did. 

Boy this game does not hold your hand at all, it is worse than real life. If you do not find the grind of life challenging enough, this game is for you.

I cant say much because spoilers can lock you out of experiencing the game, but this is the best game I have ever and probably will ever play. Don't use guides and don't watch or read anything on the game until you've finished it. If you think you have no clue how to do something go do something else and you WILL eventually figure it out. But I can't stress this enough you can't play the game properly with spoilers and you can only play it once, so take your time.

One of the most remarkable games I have ever played. Just like anyone else who will ever recommend this game, you should go into it completely blind. Idea of space camping leading to the more elegant theme and storyline of the game later on is simply magnificent.

One of, if not, the most interesting and amazing exploration-based game I've ever played in my entire life.
Even when I'm not interested in these types of games, this game managed to reel me in and revere me in a wondrous way. This game turned me into a trans lesbian queersexual pansexual astrosexual gynosexual androsexual.

This game was Out(er) of this world! It was so Wild!

(if you read this review again, you may notice something... did you catch it? There! Some of the words spell out: Outer Wilds - as in the name of the game, clever, no?)
Such a fun experiance. Abosolute gem. Love the music and exploration. Also Giant’s Deep was scary af

One of the best games I've played. The premise is great, the story is touching, the flight controls are initially challenging but ultimately rewarding and the game looks and sounds amazing. I love the feeling of freely exploring space in my own (hobbled-together) ship. This game is completely unique.

Fantastic game a real gem ... the only negative thing is by time I figured out the DLC I had already finished the game, and lost interest in it, though it looks like a very cool planet.

100% would suggest it, I agree it's better with a joystick but fun regardless I hope more people try out such a great game!

I went in totally blind and stumbled my way through, sometimes needing to take a break for a day or so until an idea popped into my head on how to proceed. Had to look up 1 thing (DLC planet I couldn't figure that out).

Amazing game but sadly lacks alot of replay ability for me... once the mystery is solved the only thing would be "How fast can you beat the game" and that doesn't interest me at all.
don't be afraid to look at guides, there are plenty of spoiler safe ones to find. great game
Can't believe I have been sitting on an ABSOLUTE GEM of a game. 100/10 absolutely recommend.
Its Beautiful. Just beautiful. Please, stay unspoiled, listen to what we all say, and just play the game. Being honest, its the only piece of media that has ever made me cry.
Frightening and beautiful at the same time. I'm still exploring...

So many people say "don't read anything!", and they're right to. But some people need to, and that's okay! Some small, first half hour spoilers below.

Outer Wilds is an exploration/puzzle game centred around [spoiler]a 22 minute time loop[/spoiler]. Wandering through the solar system, taking your time and slowly discovering the story as you go, you'll find yourself on a variety of different planets, each incredibly unique and with their own puzzles and mysteries to solve.

It's an amazing game. It'll make you laugh and smile and cry. You'll wonder how for maybe, oh, 5 minutes? And then the sun rises over Timber Hearth and you'll know exactly the vibe. You'll get it. 

I finished this game in 2021, though I've yet to find time for the DLC properly, and there's just nothing else like it out there. Nothing else that has the emotional payoff and incredible moments that has me writing a review 5 years later. Please, buy this game, and play it.

I'll see you out there.

ENG
Awesome game, the important thing is NOT to rush it or you'll lose all the magic of this game. The community is incredible, I was afraid of getting spoilers looking for stuff in guides but people respect other's experiences. Great!

ITA 
Una figata di gioco, l'importante è NON rushare senò perdi tutta la magia di questo gioco. E community incredibile, avevo paura di farmi spoiler cercando robe nelle guide invece molti rispettano l'esperienza altrui bello bello.
This game is so damn good! Really respects you to but it all together, and man, what a great ride. Was worth dodging spoilers for YEARS! Play it man, you wont regret it

Purely as a puzzle game, this is awesome. But the constant feeling of running out of time, though stressful, and the interesting mechanics are what make this game so fun to play. Also, flying a spaceship is pretty sick.
It's tempting to linger in this moment, while every possibility still exists. But unless they are collapsed by an observer, they will never be more than possibilities.
Originally got this game as a free monthly game on the PS5. It's a hidden gem. I'm a little disappointed with the expansion but the original is a masterpiece.

Saying that Outer Wilds is like a cult would not be inaccurate. Much like a cult, members of it speak of it fondly with eccstatic words about its greatness and how amazing it will be and much like a cult its members refuse to tell you any of the deeper details about it promising you greatness if only you would try.

With most cults this is a fool's errand its a lie given so you can give money to the great leader. But call me a cultist because I think I have joined. I went in pretty blind to this game and had a stellar time, some moments of frustration with puzzles that didnt feel obvious and spooks that made me uncomfortable (as someone who despises horror) but ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ if it didnt leave me with a deep sense of satisfaction and something resembling catharsis.

I have still to touch the DLC so I will see how that changes me but this is my message for all to say, go to that cult meeting. em- or well if the cult is Outer Wilds, go to it.

The feeling of curiosity from a child that never had exposure of spoilers or ruination of the internet emulates through this game. It exudes the experience of wonders of mystery that everyone once had when playing a game for the first time. Holy crap this game made me feel like a kid again. I love space.

This game is very Rad. This game also gets my Rad-commendation seal of approval.

Brilliant exploration game supported by the stunning art-style. Outer Wilds pulls you in with the gravity of the story and planets it wants you to explore.
Top 3 best games I've ever played, and that is really saying something.

This game is really one of a kind. No quest markers, no objectives, the only thing driving you is your curiosity. Every bit of detail in this game from the story to the technical side is a masterpiece. Sad I can't play this game for the first time again.

- Spoiler Free -
This ♥♥♥♥ was something else. I'm not usually a fan of space exploration and the clumsy mechanics that comes with it, but I began really getting into the groove of it towards the end. Something about seeing new places and putting the pieces together feels super satisfying, especially with the variety.
I only started playing due to it's main mechanic being spoiled for me, and the story-telling and writing was wonderful. Definitely requires some reading and some reasoning skills, in- and out-side the box.
Really damn satisfying, would lobotomize myself to experience again!

This was one of the best games I've ever played. The developers made solving the puzzles so rewarding. The way learning how to solve the puzzles built on itself throughout the whole game was great. I cannot recommend this game enough.

Simple, overall review: Game is good, fine even. 

Longer Take: Massively, and I mean super massively overhyped, if you are picking this up cause everyone online has been praising it over the years. It's a cool experience, but if you read the reviews people give this game you'll go in thinking this game is like the second coming of Christ when it's ultimately just a neat experience, so I was a little let down. It's interesting to learn about the universe, as esoteric as the game tries to be at times in both navigating and learning about it. This game can also be very frustrating, it'll tell you you've missed something, only for that thing you had to spend an extreme amount of time clambering back to to be ultimately unimportant. (The in-game hint tool will tell you if you missed a dialogue in a difficult to reach area, even if that dialogue is just a note where someone talking about how cool a rock is or something completely unimportant.)

People are correct generally in saying you should try to not spoil yourself, but if you do feel like you're bashing your head against a wall, there's no shame in looking for some hints that the community gives in guides. Overall, you could be spending your time in something far worse than Outer Wilds, so if you want a unique experience that isn't replicated in many other places, give it a shot.

If you haven't played this already. Play it. Now. Don't look up any gameplay. Don't read critic reviews. Buy it; play it; and thank me later. Saying anything about this game is saying too much, and the more you learn about it on your own, the more mind-bogglingly amazed you will be.
One of the greatest games out there. If you play it, don't look anything up and just explore everything on your own, eventually you'll piece everything together and it'll be so rewarding.

If you enjoy mysteries, puzzles, and exploring space this game is great.  It is a puzzle and mystery game so if you don't know the story already you should definitely keep it that way.  I watched a playthrough before I played and, while the game was still fun, it did take away many of the mysteries and discoveries that I could have made on my own.

There is no combat, there are threats in some areas but they are to be avoided instead of fought.
There is a time limit but every time you will know more, both you and your character remember.

finished this game and dlc about a year ago now and i'm still randomly thinking about it, I hate puzzle games and almost quit half a dozen times, if you're on the fence about buying it just get it and when you feel like putting it down after a few hours do yourself a favor and stick it out it's worth it trust, get the dlc as well it's fantastic and adds a lot to the story, 10/10 game I hated the majority of it, I think it's one of my all time favourite games and no doubt i'll still be randomly thinking about it in 10 years time

There will be no spoilers in this review.

This is simultaneously the most heartfelt experience I've ever had in a game, and genuinely one of the most immersive, involved games I've ever played. I found it randomly a few years late after seeing a YouTube short that featured it for all of 10 seconds. Thus, I downloaded it with absolutely zero idea what I was getting myself into.

How do I describe a game whose atmosphere and design are at once both incredibly gorgeous with undertones of tension? It's existential and deep, thoughtful and self-aware, Mobius Digital's first ever game, and a stellar display of what they value.

It went from a complete unknown for me, to by FAR my favorite game of all time. If you value story and total exploratory freedom, it's never too late to pick up this game. However, your weapon in this game is knowledge. If you choose to spoil yourself in any form, you are truly depriving yourself of one of the gaming industry's great gems.

This game is incredible. The music is memorizing and the visuals are breathtaking. A little hard to complete and understand without a little bit of help along the way but the payoff is unbelievable. Extremely recommended.
For how advanced the gravity and mechanics felt, it ran very smooth. I had a great time playing the game
Try to go in knowing nothing, an extremely fun game to figure out and get to the end.
This game is so peak I got stuck in a time loop irl and had to discover quantum mechanics to leave, good stuff
Fantastic game. If you know anything about it before starting, wait 5 years to forget it and then go for it. Trust me on this.

All I'm gonna say is don't look anything up to help you. Knowledge is everything. You have all the tools you need. And the search for knowledge is what makes the game fun. So by looking things up you'll end up not feeling satisfied about your finds. Hope this helps. (Also the game is really good, if you don't have it and like adventure get it).

Incredibly neat puzzle game. There is no lack of "aha!" moments and it very much rewards self-discovery by connecting the dots of the story. The Quantum Moon trolling me as I explored for about 20 hours was so hilarious and memorable, I thought the game was bugged until I figured out the mechanics. Big recommend.

¡Qué juegazo! Me hizo sentir toda una montaña rusa de emociones. Una verdadera aventura de exploración donde la historia no-lineal es una de las experiencias más únicas que he tenido. Bastante recomendable.

An incredible experience unlike any game I've played before.
Genius game design that constantly rewards the player for being curious and paying attention.
Come for the space, stay for the mystery.
I honestly don't like single-player games that often, but this one is an all time favorite omg. The game design and the story and everything is just magnificent.

I'm really liking this game. Watched my friends play it and just got it for myself. I love the sense of dread you get flying through space and exploring other planets. The best way I can explain it is comparing it to the dread R Kelly must have felt staring down years in prison for his crimes. Ya'll killin me man!

After beating the game, I sat on the floor and started crying for no clear reason.

Would recommend.

After beating the game I was like, “wow, that was incredible, I have to play another game that’s just like this!”  But now, a day after finishing it and after looking through numerous other games to fill this void, it’s only just registered that there isn’t. Outer wilds is unlike anything you’ve ever experienced. It’s once in a lifetime. So cherish it, it’s truly beautiful. It’s a masterpiece. 10/10.

Hard recommend. Great vibes, great plot. And also just a very competently made game. It's worth the time just to explore things and I think this may end up becoming a game I boot up just to hang out in the world, which genuinely doesn't happen much.
♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ man. yeah. get it. buy it. worth the money. the less you know about it the better the experience, i promise

This game is truly an amazing materpiece. It took me sometime to figure things out in the beginning and I wasn't sure of the goals or objectives until I did more exploring. I would say, that is what makes it special. You're not sure of what you're supposed to do until you start piecing things together from all the knowledge you've gathered. I find myself listening to the soundtracks of the game and getting lost in the tunes even after finishing the game. I think that sums up what kind of experience is awaiting you. I wish I could play this game for the first time again :") .
It's a very creative game while also being handcrafted, so everything look absolutely amazing

What an awesome game! Seriously, wow! It's a work of art, and I don't think I'll ever find anything like it again.

It can be a bit dull at first, but don't let that fool you. After I solved the first puzzle, I was hooked, and I think you will be too. 

For the sake of your own enjoyment, don't look up anything about this game. Just play it!

This is a fantastic space exploration game. It's a work of art and you'll be hard pressed to find anything like it again.

Do not read about it, do not watch people play it on YouTube. Go in completely blind with the least information about this game as possible, it will make it that much more enjoyable.

The very beginning can seem a little dull but it's essentially a secret tutorial. It will be completely confusing at first but keep at it. It's the moments where you piece something together that make it an amazing experience and it's a really ingenious style of storytelling

I first played and completed this game 2 years ago and went back to it recently. I remembered certain elements of the game but forgetting most of the story and the puzzles made it still enjoyable the second time round - however I'll always wish I could play this for the first again

Probably in my top 3 greatest games ever
This is one of the most unique gaming experiences I have ever come across in recent time. The game does not hold your hand through anything, and forces you to explore and challenge yourself . W game

Absolutely fantastic game! Very interesting and completely unique curiosity-driven and "knowledge based" progression, where your own understanding of the world is how you progress. The storytelling and atmosphere is really good!

 I highly recommend it, but go in blind, the less you know, the better.
One of the best games/experiences that you'll ever play. Go play it blind and don't spoil it for yourself!

The Outer Wilds ... the less you know about the more you'll love it. One day while browsing through steam and it was on sale and the name sounded familiar. so i decided to give it a shot. I went in completly blind. at first i was confused, then the more i play i got even more confused. untill it all started to make sense. BUY THIS GAME AND GO  IN COMPLETELY BLIND. the game is a life changing experience. BUY NOW









all it takes is 22 minutes ...

I absolutely love this game. Play it blind. Going in blind is paramount, as this game is entirely about curiosity and acquiring knowledge. So much so that the 'save file' is literally just your own brain IMO! Do yourself a favor and don't spoil the experience.
Fair warning, after finishing it:

1. You won't be the same.
2. You'll crave similar experiences but won't find anything even remotely close.

Honestly, the only thing that comes close to experiencing this game blind is watching someone else play it for the first time online.

I don't know what to talk about just buy the game and play it 
that is the best thing you can do for yourself instead of reading the reviews

I have recently finished and still 100% quite possibly the best story based game ever. It has been described as having a story that is the love child of project hail mary and interstellar. the people who made the game said the only thing they hate about it is they can never experience it on the same level as a player. many people have said they would kill to experience it again (me being one).
this game that quite possibly is every kind of game at once. and a reflection of human ambition on a level unparalleled.

The Outerwilds, is one of those games you can only play once.
It's has one of the best storys, and has no set linear path you have to take, It follows the story you choose to pursue, and follows the pace you choose to set, and is centered around your curitosity.
It is the best science fiction game ive ever played and give it a 10/10.

Absolute cinema.

After returning to this game after some years, it still hits the same.
Story is deep, moments are suspenseful, exploration is meaningful and the soundtrack is pure orchestra.
I dont have anything negative to say about this game. 
You can finish this game in 1 hour, or spend 20 trying to uncover everything. 

I really think that people who like to explore and enjoy a world filled with life and meaning will enjoy this plenty. 
Scientific aspects are pretty well done, though Im not some kind of a scientist to rate that.
I love that quantum mechanics are pretty much the core of this games deeper meaning.


If you're still not sure if you should play this or not, do it. Play it, experience it, you may never feel the same or look at life the same again, but its really worth it.
